2605.06614.title: SkillOS: Learning Skill Curation for Self-Evolving Agents
2605.06614.paper_summary: LLM-based agents are increasingly deployed to handle streaming tasks, yet they often remain one-off problem solvers that fail to learn from past interactions. Reusable skills distilled from experience provide a natural substrate for self-evolution, where high-quality skill curation serves as the key bottleneck. Existing approaches either rely on manual skill curation, prescribe heuristic skill operations, or train for short-horizon skill operations. However, they still struggle to learn complex long-term curation policies from indirect and delayed feedback. To tackle this challenge, we propose SkillOS, an experience-driven RL training recipe for learning skill curation in self-evolving agents. SkillOS pairs a frozen agent executor that retrieves and applies skills with a trainable skill curator that updates an external SkillRepo from accumulated experience. To provide learning signals for curation, we design composite rewards and train on grouped task streams based on skill-relevant task dependencies, where earlier trajectories update the SkillRepo, and later related tasks evaluate these updates. Across multi-turn agentic tasks and single-turn reasoning tasks, SkillOS consistently outperforms memory-free and strong memory-based baselines in both effectiveness and efficiency, with the learned skill curator generalizing across different executor backbones and task domains. Further analyses show that the learned curator produces more targeted skill use, while the skills in SkillRepo evolve into more richly structured Markdown files that encode higher-level meta-skills over time.
2605.06614.paper_summary_zh: LLM 基礎的代理人越來越多地被部署來處理串流任務，然而它們往往仍然是一次性問題解決者，無法從過去的互動中學習。從經驗中提煉出的可重用技能為自我演化提供了一個自然的基礎，其中高品質的技能策劃是關鍵瓶頸。現有的方法要麼依賴於手動技能策劃，要麼規定啟發式技能操作，或者訓練短期技能操作。然而，它們仍然難以從間接和延遲的反饋中學習複雜的長期策劃策略。為了應對這一挑戰，我們提出了 SkillOS，一種以經驗為驅動的強化學習訓練配方，用於學習自我演化代理人的技能策劃。SkillOS 將一個凍結的代理執行器與一個可訓練的技能策劃者配對，前者檢索並應用技能，後者從累積的經驗中更新外部 SkillRepo。為了提供策劃的學習信號，我們設計了綜合獎勵，並基於技能相關的任務依賴性對分組任務流進行訓練，其中早期的軌跡更新 SkillRepo，而後期相關任務評估這些更新。在多回合代理任務和單回合推理任務中，SkillOS 在效果和效率上始終超越無記憶和強記憶基準，所學的技能策劃者在不同的執行器骨幹和任務領域中具有良好的泛化能力。進一步的分析顯示，所學的策劃者產生了更具針對性的技能使用，而 SkillRepo 中的技能隨著時間的推移演變成更豐富結構的 Markdown 文件，編碼了更高層次的元技能。
2605.06614.author: Siru Ouyang et.al.
2605.06614.authors: Siru Ouyang, Jun Yan, Yanfei Chen, Rujun Han, Zifeng Wang, Bhavana Dalvi Mishra, Rui Meng, Chun-Liang Li, Yizhu Jiao, Kaiwen Zha, Maohao Shen, Vishy Tirumalashetty, George Lee, Jiawei Han, Tomas Pfister, Chen-Yu Lee
